issue:
Hello! I am trying to remove an interviewer from the platform. I am not seeing this as an option when I select the three dots next to their name. 
Can you let me know how to do this?
subject: How to Remove a User
response: To remove an interviewer, please ensure you have Company Admin or Team Admin access. Navigate to the Teams Management section, select the specific team, and go to the Users tab. You can then locate the user in the list and select the delete icon in the Action column to remove them.
justification: Replied using HackerRank guidance on managing team members and removing or locking user access.

issue: Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now.
subject: Subscription pause
response: If you are on an eligible monthly self-serve plan, you can pause your subscription instead of canceling it and resume it later when you are ready to resume hiring. This feature is designed for individual self-serve subscribers on supported monthly plans.
justification: Replied using HackerRank guidance on pausing an eligible self-serve subscription.

issue: Claude has stopped working completely, all requests are failing
subject: Claude not responding
response: I am sorry to hear you are having trouble. If this happened while using Claude Cowork, please ensure the Claude Desktop app remains open for the entire duration of your task. If the app was closed or your computer entered sleep mode, the session may have ended.
justification: Replied using Claude Cowork troubleshooting guidance for interrupted tasks.

issue: I need urgent cash but don't have any right now & only the VISA card

response: You can access cash by using your Visa card at any ATM displaying the Visa or PLUS logo. Please ensure you have your PIN ready for the transaction. You can use the Visa ATM locator tool to find the nearest available machine.
justification: Replied using Visa travel support guidance about ATM access and travel card use.

issue: I am allowing Claude to use my data to improve the models, how long will the data be used for?

response: The available support guidance here gives a specific duration only for the Development Partner Program: shared Claude Code session data may be stored for up to two years. For general model improvement, we automatically de-link your data from your user ID before any review, and access is limited to a small number of personnel involved in model training.
justification: Replied using Claude guidance on model-improvement participation and data retention.

issue: one of my employee has left. I want to remove them from our hackerrank hiring account
subject: Employee leaving the company
response: If you have Company Admin or Team Admin access, you can manage your team members by navigating to the Teams Management section. From there, select the relevant team and go to the Users tab. To remove a user from the team, find them in the list and click the delete icon in the Action column. If you prefer to simply prevent them from signing in, you can lock their account from the same Users tab, which keeps the account available for future use.
justification: Replied using HackerRank guidance on managing team members and removing or locking user access.

issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
subject: Claude for students
response: To set up the Claude LTI in Canvas, create a Claude LTI developer key in Canvas Admin > Developer Keys, install the app by Client ID under Admin > Settings > Apps, then enable Canvas under Claude for Education Organization settings > Connectors using your Canvas domain, Client ID, and Deployment ID.
justification: Replied using Claude for Education guidance on setting up the Canvas LTI integration.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend
response: In the U.S. Virgin Islands, merchants are allowed to set a minimum transaction amount of up to $10 for credit cards. However, this rule does not apply to Visa debit cards. If a merchant requires a minimum for a debit card or asks for more than $10 on a credit card, please contact your card issuer, as they manage your account and merchant policies.
justification: Replied using Visa consumer support guidance on minimum transaction amounts.
